description_long: This object is a vintage gramophone with a large, flared horn made of dark, metallic material, mounted on a hexagonal wooden base. The horn curves upward and outward, serving as the sound amplifier, and is attached to a tonearm that extends over a circular turntable. The base features decorative columns and a layered structure, with a visible crank handle on one side, suggesting manual operation. The turntable surface appears to be made of a dark, possibly marble-like material, adding to the antique aesthetic. The overall design is ornate, with a focus on classical craftsmanship and period details. This type of stereo sound system would be a striking decorative or functional piece in an urban park, vintage shop, or themed public space.
description: A vintage gramophone with a large, dark flared horn, ornate hexagonal wooden base, and a visible crank handle, evoking early 20th-century audio equipment.
description_view_0: The gramophone is seen from an angled top-down view, highlighting the flared horn, hexagonal base, and the crank handle on the right.
description_view_1: A direct front view of the gramophone, with the horn facing forward and the tonearm and turntable clearly visible.
description_view_2: Side view showing the curvature of the horn, the tonearm, and the layered structure of the wooden base.
description_view_3: Rear view dominated by the wide opening of the horn, with the back of the base and supporting columns visible.
